title: pyrus pastoralis
intro:
Pyrus pastoralis, also known as the wild pear or mountain pear, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus pastoralis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of Pyrus pastoralis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory problems and skin irritations.
